FEB-13-2009 IN 206, PLAINTIFF JOHN COYLE'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREF IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A)&(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 5/18/09 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; PLTF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTEMENT CONFERENCE DATES AND GIVE NOTICE. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. NO TESTIMONY OF PLTF AT TRIAL. IF POSITION CHANGES, PARTIES TO FILE A TIMELY NOTICED MOTION OR 30 DAYS FROM TODAY TO HAVE PLTF'S TESTIMONY OFFERED. PLTF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES/ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 21 DAYS FROM GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. IF PLTF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFT BY 4/6/09. IF DEFTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE/REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN CSR#11722

Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William M. Hake, Esq. of COOLEY MANION JONES HAKE KUROWSKI LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party Hajoca Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John Coyle

RULING:
CROSS DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hajoca Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence that Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. Mr. Ay does not provide any foundation for his apparent opinion that from 1959-1964 or 1965 gaskets that Mr. Coyle was exposed to that were supplied by defendant that were identified with no greater specificity than the words 'Cranite' or 'Garlock' contained asbestos. An order granted defendant's motion and an order re: defendant's objections and motion to strike were signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Mr. Cook creates a triable issue whether Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Cook's declaration are overruled. An order denying the motion, and an order re: defendant's evidentiary objections were signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT NEW ALBERTSON'S, INC., SUED HEREIN AS ALBERTSON'S INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant New Albertson's, Inc., SUED HEREIN AS ALBERTSON's INC.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. By not presenting responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products attributable to defendant.
